name: Sam Coslow
description: American songwriter, singer, film producer, publisher, and market analyst (1902-1982)
abstract: Sam Coslow (December 27, 1902 – April 2, 1982) was an American songwriter, singer, film producer, publisher and market analyst. Coslow was born in New York City.

works_created.t: (If You Can't Sing It) You'll Have To Swing It (Mr. Paganini)

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I met him somewhere, and he said he wanted me to record some of his tunes, and he would back me for the date. So I did that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: he said, “if you’ll make a recording of my tunes I’ll pay for the date and everything.” So he did.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, April 26, 1997
